title: Using OpenClaw on Claude? Anthropic is about to start charging you extra
summary: Anthropic will stop supporting third-party AI tools like OpenClaw for its Claude subscription. The company says users will need to pay separately for OpenClaw usage.&nbsp;

title: AI porn startup sues Apple over App Store takedowns, claims $500,000 in lost revenue
summary: AI startup Ex-Human is suing Apple for removing its apps, Botify AI and Photify AI, from the App Store. The company claims that the wrongful takedown resulted in &nbsp;$500,000 in lost revenue.&nbsp;

title: OpenAI, Anthropic contractor Mercor targeted in major security breach — what data was stolen, who carried out the hack?
summary: Founded in 2023 and regarded as one of Silicon Valley's hottest start-ups, Mercor, valued at $10 billion, works with AI companies, including OpenAI and Anthropic, to help train models by contracting specialists such as doctors, scientists, lawyers, etc across various markets, including India.

title: Is X down? Thousands of users report issues with news feed in US: What we know
summary: Elon Musk's platform X is reportedly facing another outage, preventing users from accessing feeds. As per users, issues include login failures and app crashes, marking the third disruption.&nbsp;

title: Realme 16 with iPhone Air like camera module and 7,000mAh battery launched in India, price starts at ₹31,999
summary: Realme has launched the Realme 16 in India with a 120Hz AMOLED display, 7,000mAh battery, and MediaTek Dimensity 6400 Turbo processor. The phone is at ₹31,999 and it will be available from April 5&nbsp;&nbsp;

summary: Vivo has launched the V70 FE in India with a 1.5K AMOLED display, MediaTek Dimensity 7360 Turbo processor, and IP68/IP69 ratings. The phone starts at a price of ₹37,999 and will be available for pre-order today&nbsp;

title: What is OpenAI’s ‘Spud’? Greg Brockman teases new ChatGPT model built on years of research

title: ChatGPT and Anthropic could soon route extremist AI users to a new third-party tool: Report
summary: A new tool being developed in New Zealand aims to direct individuals exhibiting violent extremist tendencies on ChatGPT to deradicalization support. The tool is being created by a company called ThroughLine.

title: Claude subscriptions no longer cover OpenClaw; users may pay extra to continue use
summary: Claude subscriptions will no longer cover third-party tools like OpenClaw. Users can still access them through pay-as-you-go bundles or a Claude API key. summary: ​​He will continue to serve as the chief mentor at Icertis while also remaining on its board, Darda said. With AI becoming more mainstream, multiple Indian startup executives are taking the entrepreneurial plunge, as the technology brings in a fundamental shift in the way businesses are run.

title: Big shakeup at OpenAI: COO Brad Lightcap shifts out of role; Fidji Simo to take medical leave
summary: OpenAI faces major leadership changes as Brad Lightcap moves roles and Fidji Simo goes on health leave. Meanwhile, the company prepares for a possible IPO, expands revenue efforts like ads, and relies on executives to maintain continuity, growth, and progress during a pivotal period.

title: Elon Musk asks SpaceX IPO banks to buy Grok AI subscriptions, NYT reports
summary: Some banks ​have agreed to spend tens of millions of dollars a ​year on the chatbot and have begun integrating it into their IT systems, the ​report said.

title: China moves to regulate digital humans, bans addictive services for children
summary: The draft regulations would ​also ban the use of other people's personal information to create digital humans without ​consent, or using virtual humans to bypass identity verification systems, reflecting Beijing's efforts to maintain control in the face of advances in artificial intelligence.

title: SpaceX delays next Starship test launch by a month, Musk says
summary: Musk posted on social media platform ‌X that ⁠the ⁠next flight of Starship's V3 vehicle was four ​to six weeks away, or in the first two ​weeks of May. Earlier, he said the first flight would take place in ​April.

title: Karnataka: Pralhad Joshi fears taxes on EVs will derail India’s emission targets
summary: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi criticized Karnataka's decision to withdraw road tax exemptions for electric vehicles, warning it could increase India's crude import bill and hinder climate goals. title: Are stricter rules holding back fintech startups? QED Investors' Nigel Morris weighs in
summary: India's fintech sector faces near-term challenges due to increased regulatory scrutiny, impacting early-stage startups' ability to scale. This tightening, coupled with global economic uncertainties, has led to portfolio companies adjusting operations and tightening underwriting standards.

title: AI needs ‘better marketing’, says Sam Altman after buying media platform TBPN
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is acquiring popular podcast The Best Possible Node (TBPN) to improve AI's public image, calling it a poorly marketed technology.
